academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree (or equivalent) with background in at least one listed field related to politics, development, management, communication, law, geography, psychology, or sociology; academic performance is also assessed.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must show proven skills in at least one of the following fields: Political Science, International Relations and Diplomacy, International Economics, Management, Cooperation and Development, Intercultural Communication, Media and Cultural Studies, International Law, Political Philosophy, Geography, Social Psychology, or Sociology.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: The Academic Committee considers the final graduation mark of the first cycle, or alternatively the average grades in the Transcript of Records. For Italian entry qualifications the minimum requirement is 95/110. If the final grade cannot be directly compared with the Italian scale, the committee considers the overall results in the candidate's study curriculum. Country-based entry title and GPA requirements also apply.

accepted_proofs: An accepted English language certificate with the minimum score required for degree programmes and study tracks held in English.
A bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification delivered entirely in English, provided the language of instruction is explicitly documented.
Native-speaker status proved by an identity document showing an eligible English-speaking nationality.
exemption_rules: Applicants who hold a degree delivered entirely in English can use that qualification to satisfy the language requirement.
Applicants considered native speakers of English under the university's nationality list can satisfy the requirement through an identity document.
Candidates who have taken a recognised test but have not yet received the certificate may submit the provisional statement of results issued by the certifying body.
language_requirements.deadline: The central university page states that deadlines are set by the relevant call for admissions; the Local Development page does not state a separate language-proof deadline [uncertain].

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Two and Call Three are only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_two: Not available to non-EU students residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available to non-EU students residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official programme page states that Call Two and Call Three are only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Required for master's degree applicants with an international entry qualification.
The central application page lists motivational letter(s) in English for master's applications.
Upload the transcript at application stage; if it is not issued in English or Italian, official translations are required.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree diploma or enrolment/provisional certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
The central page specifies an ID card for EU or equivalent applicants and a passport for others.
Required only for non-EU citizens already residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or equivalent proof
required_documents.notes: Local Development requires English at B2 level or equivalent.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into English or Italian for academic documents issued in another language
required_documents.notes: The programme page states that official translations are needed when the transcript is not available in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Listed under the master's degree enrolment documents page.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The enrolment page requires a translated and legalised transcript stating exams, grades, and programme description.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through the Universitaly portal no later than 15 July.
Required for non-EU applicants at enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Possible interview via the application platform
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants invited by the selection committee during the assessment procedure
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: No score threshold is published; the official page only states that the selection committee reserves the right to invite applicants to an interview via the application platform [uncertain].

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements
minimum requirement for Italian entry qualifications is 95/110
only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy
invite applicants to take an interview via the application platform

uncertainty_notes: The clearest 2026/27 admissions wording for Local Development was taken from the official apply.unipd.it course page, while the public unipd.it course page was used for programme identity and degree-class confirmation.
The official pages do not state a separate language-proof deadline beyond the relevant call for admissions.
Interview timing and evaluation thresholds are not specified on the official programme page.
